name: Sonny White
abstract: Ellerton Oswald White (November 11, 1917, in Panama City, Panama – April 28, 1971, in New York City), better known as Sonny White, was a jazz pianist. White took on the nickname Sonny while a member of Jesse Stone's band in the middle of the 1930s.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: And I had Sonny White playing piano. ... I think Sonny White went up there with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: there was Benny Carter, there was Kenny Clark, there was John and then there was Sonny White, and that was a great experience for me too... It was a great band too.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny White was the pianist, Wilber Kirk drums and harmonica, Wilbur de Paris, trombone... It was a really happy sounding group.

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, Kenny Clark. Yeah, Kenny Clark on drums and Sonny White on piano, was it?
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: I don't -- was it with Sonny White? ... No, it wasn't Sonny White.
